name: Read-Write Lock Manager (Non-blocking)
description: Implement a non-blocking read-write lock manager (no real threading, just bookkeeping) across two modules. Module lock_types.py must define: LockMode enum READ, WRITE; LockResult dataclass with fields acquired (bool), holders (list[str], current holders after the operation), reason (str, default ''); DeadlockError exception. Module lock_manager.py must import from lock_types.py and implement LockManager class with methods: try_acquire(resource: str, holder: str, mode: LockMode) -> LockResult; release(resource: str, holder: str) -> bool; held_by(holder: str) -> list[str] (resources currently held); resources() -> dict[str, list[str]] (mapping resource to current holders); detect_deadlock(holder: str, resource: str, mode: LockMode) -> bool checks if granting this would create a cycle in the wait-for graph. Multiple readers allowed, only one writer allowed and incompatible with any other holder. Holder may upgrade READ to WRITE only when sole holder.
test_imports: from lock_types import LockMode, LockResult, DeadlockError
signature:
# lock_types.py
from enum import Enum
from dataclasses import dataclass, field

class LockMode(Enum):
    READ = 'read'
    WRITE = 'write'

@dataclass
class LockResult:
    acquired: bool
    holders: list = field(default_factory=list)
    reason: str = ''

class DeadlockError(Exception): ...

# lock_manager.py
from lock_types import LockMode, LockResult, DeadlockError

class LockManager:
    def __init__(self): ...
    def try_acquire(self, resource: str, holder: str, mode: LockMode) -> LockResult: ...
    def release(self, resource: str, holder: str) -> bool: ...
    def held_by(self, holder: str) -> list[str]: ...
    def resources(self) -> dict: ...
    def detect_deadlock(self, holder: str, resource: str, mode: LockMode) -> bool: ...
tests:
def test_acquire_read():
    m = LockManager()
    r = m.try_acquire('file1', 'A', LockMode.READ)
    assert r.acquired is True
    assert 'A' in r.holders

def test_multiple_readers():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.READ)
    r = m.try_acquire('f', 'B', LockMode.READ)
    assert r.acquired is True
    assert sorted(r.holders) == ['A', 'B']

def test_write_excludes_readers():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.READ)
    r = m.try_acquire('f', 'B', LockMode.WRITE)
    assert r.acquired is False

def test_writer_excludes_writer():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.WRITE)
    r = m.try_acquire('f', 'B', LockMode.WRITE)
    assert r.acquired is False

def test_release_allows_others():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.WRITE)
    m.release('f', 'A')
    r = m.try_acquire('f', 'B', LockMode.WRITE)
    assert r.acquired is True

def test_held_by():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('a', 'X', LockMode.READ)
    m.try_acquire('b', 'X', LockMode.WRITE)
    held = m.held_by('X')
    assert sorted(held) == ['a', 'b']

def test_resources_view():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('a', 'X', LockMode.READ)
    m.try_acquire('a', 'Y', LockMode.READ)
    m.try_acquire('b', 'Z', LockMode.WRITE)
    resmap = m.resources()
    assert sorted(resmap['a']) == ['X', 'Y']
    assert resmap['b'] == ['Z']

def test_upgrade_sole_holder():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.READ)
    r = m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.WRITE)
    assert r.acquired is True

def test_upgrade_blocked_with_others():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.READ)
    m.try_acquire('f', 'B', LockMode.READ)
    r = m.try_acquire('f', 'A', LockMode.WRITE)
    assert r.acquired is False

def test_detect_deadlock_simple():
    m = LockManager()
    m.try_acquire('a', 'X', LockMode.WRITE)
    m.try_acquire('b', 'Y', LockMode.WRITE)
    assert m.detect_deadlock('X', 'b', LockMode.WRITE) is True or isinstance(m.detect_deadlock('X', 'b', LockMode.WRITE), bool)
constraints: Multiple READs on the same resource are compatible
WRITE is exclusive of all other holders
Re-acquire by same holder in same mode is idempotent (acquired=True, no duplicate)
Upgrade READ->WRITE only allowed when holder is the sole holder
release returns False if holder does not currently hold the resource
detect_deadlock returns a boolean and does not modify state
